texto: Whoever hurt you had a choice to hurt you or love you. It was his choice whoever he is, not yours. It is the same as how Hank made the choice to beat on me and Mom instead of loving us the way he should have. As you told me, that was not my fault either. I believe it is the option of choice that makes evil possible. When he made his choice, you really had no choice.” [said Craig]
explicacao: Orientação direta de Sarah Richards sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. A afirmação “Whoever hurt you had a choice to hurt you or love you. It was his choice whoever he is, not yours…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a amor, good-and-evil, vida, útil em momentos de vida.

frase_original: Whoever hurt you had a choice to hurt you or love you. It was his choice whoever he is, not yours. It is the same as how Hank made the choice to beat on me and Mom instead of loving us the way he should have. As you told me, that was not my fault either. I believe it is the option of choice that makes evil possible. When he made his choice, you really had no choice.” [said Craig]

texto: Remember you’ve done nothing wrong. You aren’t responsible for what has happened, but you will be responsible for what may happen if you don’t trust your parents to help.” [said Craig]
explicacao: Citação de Sarah Richards sobre temas ligados a vida. “Remember you’ve done nothing wrong. You aren’t responsible for what has happened, but you will be…” articula vida, life-lessons, trust em registro de vida, com relevância para contextos como life-lessons e trust.
